candidates.why: Explicit total revenue line; equals Net sales in preview and is a valid top-line revenue label.
Balance sheet liability, not income statement revenue.
Direct sales/revenue line and most specific operating top-line label.
Balancing row, not an actual revenue item.

variables.notes: Using a priority list for revenue label changes over time; prefer the more specific operating top-line 'Net sales' ahead of 'Total Revenue'.

REVIEWED.No COGS candidates were provided. Chose 'Raw materials and consumables' from the full preview as the clearest direct production cost line. Other rows such as 'Change in stocks of work in progress and stocks of finished goods' may also relate to cost of sales under this reporting format, so manual review is needed.

candidates.why: Valid overhead component but likely incomplete on its own.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. No explicit SG&A total row is present. Candidate list appears incomplete for overhead coverage, so selected the best overhead buckets from the full preview: personnel, external/admin-type costs, and other operating expenses. Excluded raw materials, depreciation/amortization, interest, taxes, and totals. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Employee benefit expense, [Income Statement] Other external costs

Balance sheet asset, not current-period expense.

Accumulated amortization on balance sheet, not R&D expense.
Balance sheet net asset-style line, not current-period expense.

Chose 'Equity' from the full preview because it is the actual equity line and is preferable to 'Total Equity & Minority Interest' for avoiding double counting. No separate non-controlling interest line is visible. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Balance Sheet] Equity

candidates.why: This is a combined total, not a standalone minority interest line.

variables.notes: No standalone minority interest / non-controlling interests row is present. Per rules, return empty so MIB is treated as 0; this also avoids double counting with BE.
